w:r.w:t: Do not use any company logo names in the design. Use logo that looks professional and suits the theme.
Maintain consistent brand identity across all pages.
Additional colors may be used only for indicators, metrics, or status elements.
, with limited use of brand colors in sections.
Dashboard should be the last link
Avoid adding extra CTAs to prevent user confusion.
icons for Dark Mode and RTL
Ensure button sizes are consistent from logo to the final CTA.
Dark /light mode and RTL icon should have same width and height.
Ensure consistency in shape, size, color, and behavior across all pages.
Avoid unnecessary or empty spacing, especially above the footer.
If content is center-aligned, buttons should also be center-aligned (and vice versa).
For odd numbers of items, center-align the layout.
Use structured grids (e.g., columns) instead of uneven vertical stacking.
Ensure both have distinct content while maintaining consistency.
Hover interactions on containers are not supported on mobile devices. Use alternative interactions such as tap states or subtle animations to ensure better usability.
aligned with the content and theme.
Do not appear cropped or distorted
Each main page (e.g., Service page) must:
Use supporting images to enhance understanding and engagement
on Login, Sign Up, and Dashboard pages.
for Login and Sign Up (no toggle).
Add other sign in & sign up option like relevant
Google, Apple and social login options with proper logo
Do not include demo credentials on the Sign Up page.
CTA width should match with input fields.
for better usability especially for name fields to make long entries easier.
“Remember Me” and “Forgot Password” on the same line
Place the logo inside the card
Keep RTL and Dark Mode icons at the top-right corner
Other plans should use Secondary button styles
Every page must have proper and meaningful content.
Display metrics and data where relevant and when needed.
Ensure all dashboard side nav bar sections should be unique, fully designed and developed.
